channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: trii abrirá la Bolsa de Nueva York a colombianos y peruanos
item.description: La aplicación de inversiones trii está captando el creciente apetito de los usuarios latinoamericanos por las acciones estadounidenses. En exclusiva para iupana, el cofundador de trii, Carlos Guayara, confirmó que el lanzamiento será estos últimos días de junio y que está en las etapas finales de un estudio para definir temas de última milla como el de las comisiones. Comenzó en marzo de este año en Chile, tras sellar una alianza con el broker Alpaca Trading Internacional , un acuerdo que ahora se extenderá a Colombia y Perú.

item.title: Ford Recalling 741,000 US Vehicles Due to Park System Issue
item.description: Related: Ford Recalling 255,400 US Vehicles Over Engine Issue The dealers will update the software, and inspect and replace any damaged transmission components free of charge, as per NHTSA. Ford is recalling 741,195 vehicles in the U.S. over a transmission defect that can damage the park system, which in turn could result in vehicles rolling away, the U.S.

item.title: The Price of Loyalty: How Higher Premiums Are Reshaping Carrier Retention
item.description: Alarmingly, 47% of these new buyers admit they would face serious mortgage distress if their insurance premiums spiked any higher. That’s because many policyholders have remained with the same insurer for years, choosing to prioritize convenience, multi-policy bundling discounts and loyalty perks over the potential savings from shopping around. But that era of dependable renewals and constancy with the same carrier appears to be on its way out.

item.title: GV’s Dave Munichiello On Qualcomm’s Modular Purchase, The Firm’s 10x Return And The Shift In AI Software
item.description: First, San Diego-based Qualcomm announced its acquisition of Modular, a Palo Alto, California-based software startup focused on making it easier for developers to run AI models across different types of computer chips. Watching these shifts unfold firsthand is Dave Munichiello , a managing partner at GV (Google Ventures) who led early investments and holds board seats at both Modular and SambaNova. Crunchbase News: The acquisition of Modular by Qualcomm highlights a massive push to decouple AI software from hardware fragmentation.

item.title: We Need To Save Venture Capital From Bad Data
item.description: Investing, particularly venture capital, is 50% science and 50% art. When an investor meets a promising founder and decides to take a closer look, they are handed a vast collection of data. The data is massive, but cherry-picked and packaged by the founder — the crux of the information asymmetry problem that underlies the entirety of the VC model.

item.title: Exclusive: No More Side Hustles: Why AI Startup Omnea Will Give Employees $250K To Openly Plan Their Next Startup
item.description: Created in partnership with the European angel fund Firedrop , the initiative gives Omnea employees who have completed five years of service a chance to pitch for $250,000 in seed funding to launch their own companies. Under this structure, Omnea provides $250,000 upfront with a valuation to be determined, leaving the final equity percentage open until the startup raises its next major round of capital. Omnea , a London-based artificial intelligence software company that helps businesses manage their supplier spending, is challenging traditional venture models with the launch of the Omnea Future Founders Fund.

item.title: Global and US property cat rates down 16%, APAC 19% after July renewals in 2026: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Reinsurance broker Guy Carpenter has updated its Global Property Catastrophe Rate-On-Line Index, and the latest data shows that average risk-adjusted decreases have deepened since January 1st, with property catastrophe reinsurance rate on-line (ROL) now down globally by around 16% in 2026, the steepest fall of any year since the late 1990’s. The Global Property Catastrophe Rate-On-Line Index fell by 12% at January 1st, 2026, which Guy Carpenter said at the time reflected rate-on-line decreases across global property catastrophe reinsurance contracts underwritten by reinsurers at the January renewals. “In some cases, risk-adjusted decreases have deepened since January 1, 2026 renewals, and property catastrophe rate on line (ROL) remains down globally, around 16%,” Guy Carpenter explained.

item.title: Stone Ridge mutual cat bond and ILS fund assets hit all-time high at approx $7bn
item.description: Stone Ridge Asset Management has grown assets under management across its mutual catastrophe bond, insurance-linked securities and reinsurance fund strategies by more than 8% in the last quarter of record, with additional AUM growth in the following weeks taking them to around $7 billion, a new all-time high. We believe the $7 billion of assets as of June 30th 2026 is a new all-time high for Stone Ridge Asset Management’s AUM across these mutual cat bond and ILS fund strategies. As of the last reporting date of April 30th 2026, the Stone Ridge High Yield Reinsurance Risk Premium Fund (the investment manager’s most catastrophe bond focused strategy) had reached just over $4.44 billion of assets, up from $4.14 billion at January 31st.

item.title: Life insurance capital management shifts towards tradable sidecars and secondary liquidity: McKinsey
item.description: Capital management in the life insurance sector is moving beyond balance sheet expansion to prioritise flexibility and investor alignment. However, while long-duration institutional capital remains the foundation, McKinsey & Company reports that leading platforms are now diversifying funding and exploring greater liquidity through mechanisms such as tradable sidecar interests. In a recent article, consultants at McKinsey observed that over the past decade, the US life insurance industry has undergone a “profound transformation.” “What began as opportunistic private-capital deployment into legacy annuity and life insurance blocks has evolved into a convergence of insurance carriers, alternative asset managers, and long-duration capital providers,” McKinsey said.

item.title: Rate reductions of up to 15% on loss-free business at Australia and New Zealand July 1 renewals: Howden Re
item.description: Reinsurance broker Howden Re, part of broking group Howden, reports that rate reductions of 10% to 15% on loss-free business were broadly achieved at the July 1 reinsurance renewal in Australia and New Zealand (ANZ), as buyers of protection increased their vertical limit purchases. Howden Re’s analysis reveals that some cedents actually secured larger rate reductions than 15%, as the renewal in ANZ completed amid sustained reinsurer profitability, ample capacity in the market, and an “increasingly assertive buyer community.” The report claims that the market has now clearly shifted in favour of buyers of reinsurance, with cedents “emboldened” by the softening dynamics witnessed across Asia and the US at both the April and June renewals. “That balance, diversification value alongside continued rate adequacy, underpins a reinsurer appetite that remains broad even as pricing falls,” says Howden Re.

item.title: Pool Re launches TERRA to help policyholders manage terrorism risk across their property portfolios
item.description: Britain’s largest terrorism reinsurer, Pool Re, has launched TERRA (Terrorism Risk and Resilience Assessment), designed to help policyholders assess and manage terrorism risk across their property portfolios. “The terrorist threat in the UK has been elevated for an extended period making it all the more important that policyholders have a clear understanding of their current risk exposure,” said Tom Clementi, Pool Re’s CEO. He added, “TERRA by Pool Re is designed to give businesses and insurers greater confidence in the resilience measures being implemented, while supporting a more informed approach to risk management across the market.

item.title: Latin America attracting broader and more competitive reinsurance market: Howden Re
item.description: April McLaughlin, Head of Howden Miami, commented, “Latin America is attracting a broader and more competitive reinsurance market than we have seen in some time. Howden Re has suggested that the 1 July 2026 reinsurance renewal in Latin America completed in a market defined by abundant capacity, intensifying competition and a growing appetite for structural innovation, with property catastrophe excess of loss programmes seeing rate reductions in the range of 15% to 20%. At the 1 July 2026 reinsurance renewal in Latin America, existing local players were reportedly joined by expanded interest from Bermuda, London and MGA markets, deepening a supply base that gave cedents meaningful leverage across both pricing and programme design.

item.title: Amazon Sued by Australian Consumer Watchdog Over Prime Video Ads
item.description: has been sued by Australia’s consumer watchdog for introducing advertising to its Prime Video streaming service and then forcing existing subscribers to pay more to avoid the ads. The Australian Competition & Consumer Commission said Tuesday it has commenced proceedings in the Federal Court alleging Amazon breached consumer law by including unfair contract terms in its Prime subscription contracts. It also alleges Amazon later relied on those terms to introduce advertising to Prime Video in 2024.

item.title: Supreme Court: Rastafari Can’t Sue Louisiana Prison Officials Who Cut Dreadlocks
item.description: The Supreme Court barred a former Louisiana inmate from suing prison officials who cut off his dreadlocks in violation of his Rastafari religious beliefs. When he entered the prison system, he carried a copy of an appeals court ruling in another inmate’s case holding that cutting religious prisoners’ dreadlocks violated the federal law. In a dissent, Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson wrote that state prison officials will have little incentive to abide by federal law.

item.title: US Cyber Insurance Market Sees Flat Premium, More Third-Party Claims Hit Loss Ratio
item.description: Admitted carriers carry a loss ratio of 50.2.(The paid loss ratio remains in favor of surplus lines.) AM Best said the marketplace is becoming two markets, with surplus lines writing primary and excess cyber-specific policies and “another market focused on endorsements to other commercial policies.” However, with surplus carriers looking to take the brunt of long-developing third-party claims, the “paid-loss advantage for surplus lines carriers may dissipate and possibly even turn the other way,” AM Best added. In the meantime, third-party claims are rising and total premium was basically flat after considering that a perceived increase in 2025 was caused by insurer Beazley’s move of a block of business from an offshore entity to the U.S. Related: Doing its Job: Large Majority of Cyber Losses Covered by Insurance, Says Willis Third-party claims, which are trending up 30%, will add to uncertainty of future losses because they typically have a longer tail.

item.title: Corgi Launches AI-Powered Claims TPA to Streamline Insurance Operations
item.description: AI-Powered Claims Triage At the core of Corgi Claims is an AI engine that reviews claims immediately after they are reported, automating many of the administrative tasks traditionally performed during the first notice of loss. Broad Commercial Coverage Corgi Claims supports a wide range of insurance products, including: 1. Faster answers for policyholders, cleaner files for carriers.” The launch expands Corgi’s insurance technology platform beyond underwriting and distribution, reflecting the growing adoption of AI across the insurance value chain as carriers and MGAs seek to automate claims workflows while maintaining human oversight for complex decisions.

item.title: Global Insurance Premiums Rise 7.1% as Growth Normalises, Allianz Research Finds
item.description: Global insurance premiums increased by 7.1% in 2025 , reaching €6.9 trillion , as the industry maintained strong growth despite moderating from the exceptional gains recorded the previous year, according to the Allianz Research Global Insurance Report 2026 . Asia Expected to Capture Future Growth Looking ahead, Allianz forecasts global insurance premiums will grow by an average of  5.3% annually  over the next decade. Asia Drives Life Insurance Expansion Global life insurance premiums increased  6.9%  in 2025, moderating from  11.3%  growth the previous year as North America’s annuity boom began to ease.

item.title: Vertafore’s latest AI innovations help agencies accelerate carrier statement reconciliation and turn Outlook activity into structured workflows
item.description: The post Vertafore’s latest AI innovations help agencies accelerate carrier statement reconciliation and turn Outlook activity into structured workflows appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Vertafore’s latest innovations reflect how the company is taking the insurance industry from friction to flow, eliminating distribution drag across the value chain to help agencies, MGAs and carriers achieve Distribution Velocity—speed with intentional outcomes—within and across insurance workflows. Built into Vertafore’s AgencyOne , the Velocity AI Email Agent and Velocity AI Reconciliation Agent streamline workflows that consume hours of agency staff time every day while keeping agency professionals in control for review, approval, and exception handling.

item.title: Travelers unveils proprietary insurance-focused large language model
item.description: Travelers has developed TravelersLLM, a proprietary large language model designed specifically for its property and casualty insurance business. Built by Travelers engineers and data scientists, the model was trained on millions of company documents and is intended to enhance underwriting analysis, accelerate research and model development, provide access to institutional knowledge, and improve workflows across the enterprise. According to Travelers, testing on tens of thousands of insurance-related questions showed that TravelersLLM consistently outperformed commercially available AI models, delivering higher-quality results at lower cost and greater speed.

item.title: Hippo announces enhanced partnership with Accelerant
item.description: Hippo announced an enhanced partnership with Accelerant, a risk exchange platform for the specialty insurance market. “The partnership with Hippo strengthens the Accelerant Risk Exchange for our Members and the insureds. Effective July 1, 2026, with the full program commencing October 1, 2026, Hippo will serve as a fronting carrier for more than $500 million in annual gross written premiums across Accelerant’s U.S.

item.title: Clyde & Co Survey Shows Rapid Escalation of AI, Geopolitical Risks
item.description: Technology is moving extremely fast, and a recent Clyde & Co survey of global business leaders has found that associated risks are weighing heavily. Geopolitical risks are front and center, with 72% of respondents this year saying it is having a direct commercial impact, versus about 50% saying the same in 2025. As one general counsel said in the survey, geopolitical issues were once “monitored in the background,” but today it is “directly shaping investor sentiment, buyer behavior, and confidence.” Geopolitical risk is affecting everything from the technology supply chain to regulatory compliance.

item.title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro Supplier List, Parts And Photos Exposed in Tata Data Leak
item.description: Sensitive lists of components and suppliers, and photos of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models are part of files posted on the dark web by the ransomware group that stole data from the U.S. The data maps suppliers to iPhone parts, which Apple does not disclose in its public database of suppliers, the person added. It could also upset Apple and its relationship with Tata given most of the supplier arrangements are fiercely protected by Apple, and could also hand rivals, counterfeiters and its own vendors a view of who makes what.

item.title: 10 Jurors Said Palisades Fire Suspect Isn’t Guilty. Now He Faces October Retrial.
item.description: Rinderknecht has pleaded not guilty to starting one of the most destructive wildfires in California history, and feels encouraged that so many of the jurors “resoundingly found that the government’s case was not strong, and they did not have enough evidence to convict him,” defense attorney Steve Haney said. The man accused of sparking last year’s deadly Palisades Fire will be tried again this fall after his first federal arson case ended in a mistrial Friday. Attorney Bill Essayli said they have strong evidence and will seek a guilty verdict in a new trial.

item.title: Igloo expands footprint with Eazy Digital acquisition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Cedents secure rate cuts, better terms, structural improvements at 1.7: Howden Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AIG appoints Brian Jee as head of financial lines in Singapore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA and the Bank of England set out approach to joint regulation of systemic stablecoin issuers
item.description: The Bank of England and the FCA have published a joint approach setting out how they and where relevant other authorities will work together to regulate systemic stablecoin issuers in the UK. It explains how responsibilities will be split between the authorities, and how UK stablecoin issuers may move from FCA supervision to joint regulation once recognised as systemic by HM Treasury. The approach aims to provide clarity and predictability for firms as the market develops.

item.title: FCA sets landmark crypto rules to cement the UK’s place as a global hub
item.description: Firms supporting people to buy, trade and hold crypto will need to meet clear standards under landmark rules set out by the FCA. The new framework also sets out specific rules for stablecoins, a type of cryptoasset designed to maintain a stable value, typically by being linked to a currency such as the pound. Following consultation, the FCA has simplified key elements of the regime to make it more workable in practice including simpler capital requirements for stablecoin firms and tailoring trading rules to better reflect how crypto markets operate.

item.title: Loss free Aus and NZ reinsurance renewals fall 10-15%, cyclone risk less material: Howden Re
item.description: The broker explained that the role of cyclone risk has become less material in reinsurance programmes in Australia and New Zealand, with the Australian government backed cyclone reinsurance pool an influencing factor at the renewals. But cyclone risk has moved to be viewed as a peril that is “demonstrably less material” according to Howden Re, citing the Australian governments cyclone reinsurance pool as a key factor that has “meaningfully altered the peril hierarchy for a number of cedents.” At the July 1st 2026 reinsurance renewals loss free business in Australia and New Zealand saw rate reductions of between 10% and 15%, as sharpened reinsurer focus and more active overseas capacity softened the market, according to broker Howden Re.

item.title: Aon appoints Yue Yang as Managing Director, Cyber Solutions EMEA
item.description: Aon, a global insurance and reinsurance broker and risk advisor, has announced the appointment of Yue Yang as Managing Director, Cyber Solutions EMEA. David Molony, Head of Cyber Solutions EMEA at Aon, commented, “Yue’s deep expertise across cyber insurance and security, combined with her strong advisory track record, will be instrumental as we continue to strengthen and evolve our cyber capabilities at Aon.” Yang brings more than 15 years of industry experience, with a background spanning cyber broking and management consulting.

item.title: Euna strengthens healthcare underwriting team
item.description: Specialist managing general agent (MGA) Euna has strengthened its healthcare underwriting team with the appointments of Sharon Brennan as Senior Healthcare Underwriter and Rosie David as Healthcare Underwriter, as the company continues to expand its medical malpractice offering in response to growing demand for specialist healthcare insurance. The appointments follow a period of strong performance for Euna’s healthcare portfolio and form part of the company’s ongoing investment in underwriting expertise to support its continued growth. Rosie and Sharon will help us continue to elevate our proposition, deepen our underwriting capability and deliver the first-rate service brokers expect from Euna.” Brennan joins Euna with more than 25 years of healthcare underwriting experience across UK and international markets, most recently serving as Practice Leader in the London Market.

item.title: Guy Carpenter expects reinsurance industry to generate ROE of 16.6% in 2026
item.description: Guy Carpenter, the reinsurance broking arm of Marsh, expects reinsurers to continue delivering profitable returns in 2026, estimating that the reinsurance industry will generate a return on equity (ROE) of 16.6% for the year. Reported in its July 2026 Reinsurance Renewal Report, Guy Carpenter’s estimate of a 16.6% return on equity is down from 18.9% in 2025, slightly up from 16.4% in 2024, and well below the hard market peak of 21.9% in 2023. The chart below, taken from Guy Carpenter’s July 1 2026 reinsurance renewal report, shows reinsurer profitability since 2017, plotting the ROE against the cost of equity.

item.title: DOJ Closes Criminal Probe Into Abbott Over Baby Formula Plant, WSJ Reports
item.description: Justice Department closed a criminal probe into Abbott Laboratories over its handling of a baby formula plant linked to potentially deadly bacteria and infant deaths, opting instead for civil penalties, the Wall Street Journal reported on Sunday. Abbott initiated a recall of its infant formula products and closed its Michigan plant in 2022 after investigators found traces of a potentially deadly bacteria at that plant. The recall and the plant closure worsened a national shortage of baby formula that had begun with pandemic supply chain issues.

item.title: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. Acquires Cincinnati Benefit Solutions
item.description: Drew Locaputo and the Cincinnati Benefit Solutions team will remain in their current location under the direction of Brian Lomas, head of Gallagher’s Great Lakes region employee benefits consulting and brokerage operations. announced the acquisition of Ohio-based Cincinnati Benefit Solutions. Cincinnati Benefit Solutions provides employee benefits services for small businesses in Cincinnati and nearby areas.

item.title: Texas Reaches $34M Settlement With AstraZeneca for Illegal Kickback Scheme
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton secured a settlement with AstraZeneca Pharmaceuticals to resolve claims that the company engaged in an illegal kickback scheme that improperly influenced prescriptions paid for by Texas Medicaid. In many cases, these prescriptions were covered by Medicaid, resulting in millions of dollars in claims to Texas Medicaid that were tainted by AstraZeneca’s illegal inducements. Attorney General Paxton sued AstraZeneca under the Texas Health Care Program Fraud Prevention Act (“THFPA”).

item.title: U.S. and World Bank Creating Political Risk Insurance for Ukraine Fund
item.description: International Development Finance Corporation has agreed with the World Bank’s guarantee arm MIGA to create a political risk insurance framework for a U.S.-Ukrainian reconstruction investment fund. reduce the risk so that the private sector can feel confident about the opportunity to invest in Ukraine’s future,” World Bank President Ajay Banga said at the signing. Ed Mountfield, vice president and chief financial officer of the World Bank’s Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency, who signed the deal with Coleman, said the war-risk coverage would help mobilize private capital investments and strengthen Ukraine’s overall business environment.

item.title: ‘We’re in for the long haul’: Valueattics Re targets steady growth with domestic gains, regional moves
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Claims AI ROI Should Be a Scale Test, Not a Spreadsheet
item.description: Claims leaders should read those stories together. ROI should be designed before the pilot begins because it forces a claims organization to define what success would actually mean. If insurance products are already adapting to exposures like hallucination and model drift, claims organizations should not pretend those issues are abstract.

item.title: Allstate expands SEC sponsorship with focus on women’s sports
item.description: The Southeastern Conference (SEC) and Allstate have extended their multi-year sponsorship agreement, with the insurer placing a greater emphasis on women’s athletics across the conference. The expanded partnership introduces the Allstate SEC Women’s Champions Cup, a year-long competition spanning 10 women’s sports across all 16 SEC universities. The SEC, founded in 1933 and now comprising 16 universities following the additions of Oklahoma and Texas in 2024, said the expanded sponsorship will provide additional resources to support the growth of women’s sports programs across the conference.

item.title: Federato Expands Into APAC, Japan
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Federato (San Francisco) has expanded into Asia-Pacific and Japan, opening an office in Sydney and naming Roland Slee Regional VP, Asia Pacific and Japan. Federato says its APAC and Japan expansion is intended to bring its insurance platform to insurers seeking to apply AI more directly to underwriting and claims operations. “Federato is fully committed to serving insurers across APAC and Japan, and Roland Slee is key to this effort,” comments Will Ross, Co-Founder and CEO, Federato.

item.title: Aon Names Mike Reid EMEA Facultative Leader
item.description: “Mike is a great example of this approach, and we are excited to have him lead our EMEA Facultative Reinsurance team.” “Aon is a recognized leader in the facultative sector and this role is a compelling opportunity to apply my experience and relationships to deliver innovative solutions to clients,” comments Reid. (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Aon (Dublin) has appointed Mike Reid EMEA CEO of Facultative Reinsurance, effective July 1, 2026. Mike Reid, EMEA CEO, Facultative Reinsurance, Aon.

item.title: PE-backed Insurance Broker Hub International Files Confidentially for U.S. IPO
item.description: Private equity-backed insurance broker Hub International announced on Friday that it has confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the United States. Within the insurance sector, brokers have dominated recent listings, accounting for six of the 16 largest IPOs since 2021, led by Ryan Specialty Holdings, according to S&P Global. Analysts note that insurance brokers face less volatility than underwriters when going public.

item.title: More Americans Expected to Travel Over July 4th Holiday
item.description: Nearly 72.2 million Americans will travel at least 50 miles from home for Independence Day between Saturday, June 27 and Sunday, July 5. Independence Day Travelers by Mode of Transportation By Car: AAA projects 61.4 million people will travel by car over the July 4th week, nearly the same number as last year when 61.3 million travelers took road trips. This year’s domestic travel forecast surpasses last year’s record of 71.8 million travelers but is smaller than recent year-over-year gains, with cruises the only mode of transportation seeing significant gains.

item.title: Economic and insured losses from June US SCS outbreak likely to reach low single-digit billions: Aon
item.description: Preliminary estimates indicate that combined economic and insured losses from severe convective storm (SCS) outbreaks in the United States between June 18-25, 2026, will likely land in the low single-digit billions USD, according to broking group Aon’s Weekly Cat Report. Another key factor for this severe weather was the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur, which produced a regional tornado outbreak with numerous weak-to-moderate tornadoes in Louisiana and Mississippi, while a subsequent episode from June 21–23 featured stronger tornadoes and a long-track derecho from Kansas into Oklahoma and Arkansas. Between June 21–23, a separate outbreak of severe weather and tornadoes occurred across parts of Kansas, Illinois, and Indiana.

item.title: Texas Industrial Facility Faces $3.5M in Fines for Safety Violations
item.description: Department of Labor has proposed more than $3.5 million in fines against three companies after federal inspectors determined they failed to protect workers during post-emergency response cleanup after a chemical spill at the BWC Terminals industrial facility east of Houston, Texas. faces $392,501 in proposed penalties for two willful and five serious violations that include a lack of training, a safety and health program, an emergency response plan for hazardous waste operations and emergency response, and deficiencies related to use of respirators. 27, 2025, sulfuric acid spill at the BWC Terminals LLC industrial facility in Channelview led to multiple employee injuries.

item.title: ANV Agrees to Acquire Specialist MGA Assured Underwriting Group
item.description: ANV Group Holdings Ltd., a global insurance intermediary platform, announced it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Assured Underwriting Group Ltd. We are confident this is the right home for our business, our people, and our clients.” In connection to the acquisition, Grant Thornton is serving as financial adviser for Assured Underwriting Group, while Clyde & Co is acting as AUG’s legal counsel to Assured Underwriting Group. (AUG), a specialist MGA providing insurance-backed financial protection products to tour operators and the travel sector across the United Kingdom and Europe.

item.title: Texas Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit by Survivors of Uvalde School Shooting
item.description: The Texas Supreme Court on Friday rejected an appeal by Robb Elementary students and teachers who argued failures by the Department of Public Safety and Uvalde County contributed to a botched response to the 2022 shooting that killed 19 students and two teachers. All were traumatized.” The lawsuit argued that DPS and Uvalde County failed to protect students and teachers — an allegation, the court acknowledged, “we quite frankly cannot disagree with.” Even so, the appeals court said, their lawsuit argued that DPS and the county acted negligently under the Texas Tort Claims Act — a law that did not apply to their claims. The court’s order, issued without comment, upheld a February ruling by the San Antonio-based 4th Court of Appeals that dismissed the lawsuit.

item.title: Ageas UK Expands Distribution with New Home Insurance Partnership with Source Insurance
item.description: “We’re looking forward to building a strong relationship together and supporting Source in delivering smooth, customer-focused insurance solutions.” The post Ageas UK Expands Distribution with New Home Insurance Partnership with Source Insurance appeared first on Insurtech Insights . “Ageas brings great underwriting expertise, market presence and commitment to customer outcomes and we’re looking forward to working with them.” Strengthening Adviser Support The Source agreement builds on a series of recent distribution initiatives by Ageas UK, including an embedded insurance partnership with an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) insurer and support for a new embedded motor insurance proposition launched by electric vehicle manufacturer BYD. Claire Green, Distribution Director at Ageas UK, said the insurer is pleased to partner with Source as it continues to grow its home insurance panel.

item.title: What Insurance Leaders Get Wrong About Culture
item.description: When people think about a healthy culture in most insurance organizations, they usually say the same things: People follow the process, leaders avoid trouble, and long-tenured employees are promoted. Insurance leaders often mistake compliance for culture. When AI shifts what the work actually requires, carriers that have spent years measuring culture through stability metrics cannot answer basic questions: what does good behavior look like to support and sustain change in the organization and the industry overall?

item.title: Corgi launches Corgi Claims
item.description: Corgi announced the launch of Corgi Claims, a full-service third-party administrator. Corgi Claims handles every major line of business, including commercial liability, property, catastrophe, renters, trucking, workers’ compensation, and specialty programs. Corgi Claims is available for insurance carriers, MGAs, captives, program administrators, and self-insured organizations.
